items.prompt: MAR (missing at random) means missingness depends on:
choices: Observed variables but not the missing value after conditioning on observed data
items.explain: MAR allows modeling missingness using observed columns, unlike MNAR.

items.prompt: Example 5.10 (high earners not reporting income) illustrates:
items.explain: Missingness depends on the unobserved income value.

items.prompt: Sensor file truncation is one cause of missing data mentioned in the chapter.
items.explain: Hardware or disk failures can truncate logs (Example 5.13).

choices: It determines which repair strategies are defensible
It picks the ML algorithm automatically
It eliminates the need for documentation
items.explain: Mechanism assumptions dictate whether deletion or simple imputation is appropriate.
